name: Introduction to Basic Gaming – Game Settings, Rendering Techniques & Game Engines

questions.question: What does resolution in game settings affect?
What is the main purpose of a game engine?

What do shaders mainly control in a game?
Which of these is a popular game engine?
What is the primary benefit of technologies like ray tracing and DLSS in modern games?
questions.option1: More realistic lighting with improved performance using AI upscaling
questions.option2: Faster internet speed for online gaming

description:
This course provides a foundational understanding of modern game development, focusing on the core systems that power interactive digital experiences. It is designed for beginners who want to explore how games are built, optimized, and rendered across platforms.

Students will start by learning essential game settings and configurations, including resolution scaling, frame rate control, graphics quality tuning, and performance optimization techniques. The course then dives into rendering technologies, covering key concepts such as rasterization, lighting models, shaders, textures, and real-time rendering pipelines used in modern games.

The final section introduces popular game engines like Unity and Unreal Engine, helping learners understand how engines handle physics, scripting, asset management, and scene creation. Practical demonstrations and mini-projects guide students in applying these concepts to create simple interactive environments.

By the end of the course, learners will have a clear understanding of how game settings impact performance, how rendering brings visuals to life, and how game engines integrate all components into a playable experience—building a strong base for further study in game development.

name: Advanced Game Development – Real-Time Rendering, Engine Architecture & Performance Optimization

questions.question: In deferred rendering, what is stored in the G-buffer?
questions.option1: Geometry and material data like normals, albedo, depth

questions.question: What is the main drawback of real-time ray tracing compared to rasterization?
What is the role of frustum culling in rendering?
questions.option2: Removing objects outside the camera view

questions.question: What does DLSS primarily use to upscale images?
What is frame pacing concerned with?

description:
This course is designed for learners who already understand the basics of game development and want to dive into the advanced systems behind modern AAA games. It explores how high-performance engines and cutting-edge graphics technologies work together to deliver realistic and scalable gaming experiences.

The course begins with advanced rendering pipelines, including deferred and forward rendering, physically based rendering (PBR), global illumination, and real-time ray tracing. Students will explore how modern GPUs handle complex lighting, shadows, reflections, and post-processing effects. It also covers AI-powered technologies like DLSS (Deep Learning Super Sampling) for performance optimization without sacrificing visual fidelity.

Next, learners will study engine architecture, including memory management, multithreading, ECS (Entity Component System), asset streaming, and scene management. The course explains how professional engines like Unreal Engine and Unity structure their systems for scalability and efficiency.

A strong emphasis is placed on performance optimization, where students learn CPU/GPU profiling, bottleneck analysis, LOD systems, culling techniques, and frame pacing. Real-world case studies help illustrate how large-scale games maintain smooth performance across different hardware.

By the end of the course, learners will be able to understand and analyze modern rendering systems, optimize complex scenes, and work with advanced engine-level concepts—preparing them for roles in high-end game development or graphics programming.

name: Advanced Game Performance & Graphics Optimization – GPUs, Frame Generation & Game Settings

questions.option2: GPU is fully utilized while CPU waits

description:
This course dives deep into the technical side of gaming performance, focusing on how hardware and software settings interact to deliver smooth and visually rich gameplay. It is ideal for learners who want to understand how to optimize games at a system and engine level.

The course begins with a detailed study of GPU architecture, explaining how modern graphics cards process rendering workloads, manage VRAM, and execute parallel computations. Students will learn how different GPU components contribute to rendering, shading, and post-processing.

Next, the course explores advanced game settings, including texture quality, anti-aliasing techniques, shadow resolution, anisotropic filtering, and dynamic resolution scaling. It emphasizes how each setting impacts both CPU and GPU performance, helping learners make informed optimization decisions.

A major focus is placed on modern performance technologies such as frame generation and AI upscaling. Students will examine how tools like NVIDIA DLSS, AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution, and NVIDIA Reflex improve frame rates, reduce latency, and enhance visual quality. Concepts like frame interpolation, motion vectors, and latency trade-offs are explained in detail.

The course also covers CPU-GPU interaction, bottleneck identification, frame timing, and system-level optimization. Learners will explore profiling tools and techniques to analyze performance issues in real-world scenarios.

By the end of the course, students will have a strong understanding of how GPUs, game settings, and modern rendering technologies work together—enabling them to optimize gameplay performance, fine-tune graphical settings, and understand the hardware-level behavior of modern games.

description:
This course is designed for beginners who want to explore the art of dance and develop confidence in movement and expression. It focuses on building a strong foundation in rhythm, coordination, and body control while introducing learners to different dance styles.

Students will start with basic movement techniques, including posture, balance, footwork, and timing. The course then progresses into understanding rhythm and musicality, helping learners synchronize their movements with beats and patterns in music.

Learners will also explore a mix of popular dance styles such as contemporary, freestyle, and basic hip-hop. Inspired by global influences and artists like Michael Jackson, the course encourages creativity and personal expression through movement.

The course includes guided practice sessions, short choreography routines, and exercises to improve flexibility and stamina. Emphasis is placed on confidence-building, stage presence, and enjoying the process of dancing.

By the end of the course, learners will be able to perform simple routines, understand rhythm and movement flow, and express themselves more freely through dance.
